game_name: Wizardry II: The Knight of Diamonds

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Characters must be level 13+ from previous game or pre-generated; no tutorial area provided
Immediate high difficulty; baptism by fire for under-leveled parties
One piece per level; mandatory progression gate for story completion
Parallel objectives to armor collection; each level has distinct quest content
Mechanical convenience; differs from first game's forced dungeon exit requirement

typical_tension_triggers: High difficulty curve from start (level 13+ requirement)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Front-loaded difficulty with sustained pressure; players face baptism by fire immediately with no training grounds or safe zones; tension maintained through mandatory exploration of all levels without skip options

difficulty_curve.early_game: Extreme difficulty spike at start; assumes level 13+ characters; no mercy for under-leveled parties
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Sustained high difficulty maintained through dungeon depth; all levels mandatory
difficulty_curve.late_game: Difficulty maintained; final armor pieces require completion of all preceding levels
notable_spikes: Game entry: immediate baptism by fire with no training area
Mandatory exploration: cannot skip levels to avoid content

notable_level_patterns.description: One Knight of Diamonds armor piece hidden in each of 6 levels; all pieces required for story completion; forces exhaustive exploration of every level
Each dungeon level contains distinct quest objectives beyond armor collection; quests provide structure and varied objectives per level
Unlike first game, no elevators or shortcuts to bypass levels; dungeon sequence is strictly linear, requiring exploration of all 6 levels in order
